text-opt-bm

Benchmarking LLMs on text optimization

text-opt-bm gives an LLM agent a weak Python program and a scoring function. The agent repeatedly edits the program, and the benchmark records the best valid score it finds.

  • scoresMetrics include traced memory, compressed bytes, executed bytecode, and error rate.
  • perfect informationFour tasks are scored on the same workload the agent optimizes.
  • generalizationFour base tasks expose training data and keep a larger sealed test; three have completed campaign results below.
  • ML systemsThree research-oriented tasks add routing, optimizer transfer, and physically constrained SLM compression.
  • plotsCurves step at accepted submissions. Select any task card for protocol details.

Reasoning effort and model sweep

  • tasksThe four retained perfect-information tasks.
  • settingsgpt-5.5 with high, low, and no reasoning effort; grok-4.5 with xhigh reasoning through Cursor.
  • runsFive one-hour runs per task and setting.
  • scopeSeparate gpt-5.5 xhigh saturation probes are excluded from these plots.
  • figureScores are normalized per task: 1 is the seed program, 0 is the best score found within the plotted runs. Bands show one standard deviation of the benchmark aggregate over independent per-task run choices.
  • time axisOptimizer-active time from 0 to 60 minutes. A run the campaign launcher interrupted and relaunched is stitched at the interruption point, so improvements appear at the active minute they occurred; nothing is clamped to the 60-minute mark.
Four perfect-information tasks, averaged by model and reasoning setting
gpt-5.5 highgpt-5.5 lowgpt-5.5 nonegrok-4.5 xhigh
Reading the bands: shaded regions are ±1 standard deviation of the benchmark aggregate over independent per-task run choices.
Every perfect-information task, 20 runs each (5 per setting), raw scores
gpt-5.5 highgpt-5.5 lowgpt-5.5 nonegrok-4.5 xhigh
individual runsetting mean
Task details: select any task card to reveal its objective and scoring method.

Training feedback and sealed test

  • tasksThe three campaign-tested generalization tasks.
  • gradingDuring optimization, the visible training set supplies the score.
  • testThe same selected programs are evaluated afterward on a sealed test.
  • settingsgpt-5.5 high, low, and none; grok-4.5 xhigh through Cursor.
  • scopecompress_heldout curves use the repaired plain-buffer scorer and a complete offline rescore of every featured submission.
  • figureLeft panel: train error. Right panel: sealed test error. Aggregate curves are normalized per task and averaged across the three tasks.
  • runsFive independent one-hour runs per task and model setting.
  • time axisOptimizer-active time from 0 to 60 minutes. A run the campaign launcher interrupted and relaunched is stitched at the interruption point, so improvements appear at the active minute they occurred; nothing is clamped to the 60-minute mark.
Three generalization tasks, train set versus sealed test
Training · graded
Sealed test
gpt-5.5 highgpt-5.5 lowgpt-5.5 nonegrok-4.5 xhigh
Per task, train (graded) and sealed test, raw scores
gpt-5.5 highgpt-5.5 lowgpt-5.5 nonegrok-4.5 xhigh
individual runsetting mean

Visible training feedback versus hidden validation

  • tasksThe same three generalization tasks as the training-feedback comparison.
  • visibleThe graded set is the full visible training set.
  • hiddenThe agent sees five examples and receives only an aggregate validation score on unseen data.
  • figureBoth conditions use the same sealed test. The three panels show train score, validation score, and sealed test score.
  • runsFive independent one-hour runs per task and feedback condition.
  • time axisOptimizer-active time from 0 to 60 minutes. A run the campaign launcher interrupted and relaunched is stitched at the interruption point, so improvements appear at the active minute they occurred; nothing is clamped to the 60-minute mark.
Three generalization tasks, train, validation, and sealed test
Training
Hidden validation · graded
Sealed test
visible train gradinghidden validation grading
Per task, train, hidden validation (graded for the hidden arm), and sealed test, raw scores
visible train gradinghidden validation grading
individual runcondition mean

Training-set size sweep

  • tasksThe three generalization tasks with fixed distributions and fixed sealed tests.
  • sizesVisible train-to-test ratios are 1:4, 1:8, and 1:16.
  • figureLeft panel: train error. Right panel: sealed test error for the submitted programs.
  • runsFive independent one-hour runs per task and training-set size.
  • time axisOptimizer-active time from 0 to 60 minutes. A run the campaign launcher interrupted and relaunched is stitched at the interruption point, so improvements appear at the active minute they occurred; nothing is clamped to the 60-minute mark.
Three generalization tasks, train-set size sweep
Training · graded
Sealed test
1:4 (largest train)1:81:16 (smallest)
Per task, train (graded) and sealed test, one line per training-set size, raw scores
1:4 (largest train)1:81:16 (smallest)
individual runsize mean

Research-oriented ML systems optimization

Curve semantics: faint step traces are independent trials; the bold trace is their pointwise mean. Dashed horizontal lines are fixed reference baselines named in each subplot's key.
